meta.notes: Read-only research log. No trades are ever placed by this routine.

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: 78 of 80 required events reported as of company's May 11, 2026 update; final analysis triggers on 80th event; topline readout guided for 2H 2026
catalyst.next_check: watch for 8-K / press release announcing 80th event

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 69.8% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold

catalyst.name: $25B investment-grade bond deal (priced late Jun/early Jul 2026, $89B demand, proceeds retired bridge loan) + staggered IPO lockup
catalyst.status: First lockup tranche (20% of 180-day block) unlocks 2nd full trading day after Q2 2026 earnings, expected late Jul/early Aug 2026; further 7% tranches Aug-Oct; full lockup expires Dec 8 2026
catalyst.next_check: Q2 2026 earnings date announcement; stock reaction into lockup

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within cap but second-largest single-name risk; lockup supply event ~2-3 weeks out

flags: SLS at 69.8% of NAV is nearly double the 35% single-name trim threshold, in a pre-binary-catalyst biotech with no news that breaks the thesis but no catalyst confirmation either.
Zero USD cash buffer - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).
SPCX facing its first staggered lockup unlock in ~2-3 weeks; stock already down 4-5% pre-market with no negative credit news (bond was oversubscribed, investment-grade).

runs.note: Run date is a Saturday; markets closed. All marks are Fri 2026-07-17 official close; gap_pct is vs Thu 2026-07-16 close. This is weekend prep for Monday 2026-07-20 open.

catalyst.name: Phase 3 REGAL trial (Galinpepimut-S, AML) - event-driven unblind
catalyst.status: Still 78 of 80 required events per company's last confirmed update; CEO Stergiou posted early-July that this 'may likely be my last brief posting around REGAL' before the 80th event triggers quiet period. No 80th-event/unblind announcement found in this search.
catalyst.news_since_last_run: CEO publicly rebutted investor/short-seller criticism of REGAL trial design (control-arm setup, event-accrual pace, non-disclosure of blinded metrics) in a public post ('let's separate the noise from the data'). Fri 7/17 stock rallied +12% intraday (open 11.88 -> close 13.30) on elevated volume with no confirmed press release/8-K found explaining the move - unverified, may be readout-anticipation or rumor-driven.
catalyst.next_check: watch for 8-K / press release announcing 80th event and quiet-period start; verify Friday's rally against any Monday follow-through or reversal

positions.risk_flag: OVER_CONCENTRATED - 73.8% of NAV vs 35% trim threshold, worse than last run (69.8%); unexplained +12% single-day move ahead of a binary readout with no confirmed catalyst news

catalyst.name: $25B investment-grade bond deal (closed, retired bridge loan) + Nasdaq-100/Russell 1000 index inclusion inflows + staggered IPO lockup
catalyst.status: S&P/Moody's/Fitch all confirmed low-tier investment grade (BBB/Baa1/BBB+) on 6/18/26, but SPCX bonds trade at a wider credit spread (1.62pp over Treasuries) than the average BB junk bond (1.55pp) as of early July - credit market pricing more risk than the raters. Joined Nasdaq-100 on 7/7 (~$4.3B forced inflow) plus ~$3B from Russell 1000 fast-track, but float is lockup-constrained. First 20% lockup tranche unlocks ~2nd trading day after Q2 earnings (expected ~Aug 6), then further 7% tranches ~Aug 21 and ~Sep 10; combined with other unlock triggers, reporting suggests up to ~44% of shares (~$123B) could become sellable by early September - a materially larger overhang than modeled in the prior run.
catalyst.news_since_last_run: Stock down another ~14.9% since 7/11 mark, closed Fri near session low after fading from an intraday high of 130.33. No negative operational news found; move consistent with bond-market credit skepticism + lockup-overhang repricing ahead of index-inflow support.
catalyst.next_check: Q2 earnings date confirmation (sets Aug 6ish first-unlock trigger), credit spread trend, index-inflow timing vs lockup supply

positions.risk_flag: WATCH - within cap and NAV% eased to 26.0% (was 30.1%) purely because SLS grew faster, not because SPCX is de-risking; credit spreads wider than junk despite fresh IG rating; lockup overhang bigger than previously modeled

flags: Run falls on a Saturday - markets closed; all data is Friday 7/17 close vs Thursday 7/16 close, prepping for Monday 7/20 open, not same-day premarket.

SLS concentration worsened to 73.8% of NAV (vs 69.8% last run), more than double the 35% single-name trim threshold, on a stock that just moved +12% in a single session with no confirmed news catalyst found - verify before Monday open.

SLS CEO went public defending REGAL trial design against investor/short-seller criticism, signaling this is now a contested narrative right before the quiet period begins - credibility risk to monitor alongside the data risk.

SPCX lockup overhang is meaningfully larger than what was logged last run: reporting now points to ~44% of shares (~$123B) potentially unlocking by early September versus the prior simple 20%-then-7%-tranches picture.

SPCX credit spreads (1.62pp over Treasuries) are trading wider than the average junk bond (1.55pp) despite SpaceX's fresh investment-grade ratings from all three agencies - a bond-market signal at odds with the equity story.
Zero USD cash buffer persists - account fully invested, no dry powder to add on weakness (not on margin, so no forced-liquidation risk).
CING is not currently a held position in this account - skipped per-ticker strategy block; flagging in case it was expected to be tracked.
